gene_region.label: HTR1A transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical HTR1A genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: HTR1A encodes the serotonin 1A receptor regulating anxiety circuits, mood pharmacology, thermoregulation, and autoreceptor feedback. This topic bundle emphasizes obscure high-impact biology, with interpretation centered on rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local HTR1A bundle is oriented toward obscure high-impact biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: HTR1A variants can alter, tag, or contextualize rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Obscure or underappreciated genes with surprising phenotype impact
Rare disease, trait modifier, sensory, metabolic, immune, digestive, and neurobehavioral pathway review
Candidate-gene triage where exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, and phenotype can flip interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: HTR1A methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for HTR1A.
Because HTR1A biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use HTR1A promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
